task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher dashboard is visible in the browser, indicating successful login with the provided admin credentials.

task_completion.description: Inspect the 'data-fetcher' deployment in the 'app-tier' namespace
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI or terminal shows the 'data-fetcher' workload in the 'app-tier' namespace, initially displaying a CrashLoopBackOff state.
The Network Policies list in the Rancher UI or terminal output shows that the original default-deny policies for 'app-tier' and 'cache-tier' are still present and have not been deleted.

task_completion.description: Apply least-privilege NetworkPolicy for api.github.com and DNS
task_completion.visual_evidence: A NetworkPolicy YAML or UI configuration is visible showing egress rules specifically allowing DNS (port 53) and HTTPS (port 443) or explicit FQDN rules for api.github.com for the 'data-fetcher' pods.

task_completion.description: Apply least-privilege NetworkPolicy for redis-cache connectivity
task_completion.visual_evidence: A NetworkPolicy YAML or UI configuration is visible showing egress from 'data-fetcher' to 'redis-cache' (port 6379) and/or ingress on 'redis-cache' from 'data-fetcher'.
The 'data-fetcher' pod or deployment is shown in a 'Running' or 'Active' state (e.g., 1/1 ready) in the Rancher UI or via kubectl, indicating the CrashLoopBackOff is successfully resolved.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Kubernetes environment (Rancher UI or kubectl)
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine interaction with the Rancher web interface or a terminal running kubectl commands, not just a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not use an 'allow-all' shortcut to bypass least-privilege constraints
integrity.visual_evidence: The applied NetworkPolicies contain specific podSelectors, namespaceSelectors, or ports, rather than empty `{}` ingress/egress rules that allow all traffic.

integrity.description: Agent did not delete the default-deny policies
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no commands like `kubectl delete networkpolicy default-deny` or UI actions deleting the pre-existing deny policies.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespaces are 'app-tier' and 'cache-tier'. Associated workloads are 'data-fetcher' and 'redis-cache'. The task requires resolving a CrashLoopBackOff by adding least-privilege NetworkPolicies for DNS, api.github.com, and redis-cache, strictly without deleting the existing default-deny policies.
